mnemonics.title: Divide and Conquer Steps
mnemonics.content: DCC: Divide, Conquer, Combine - Just like "Don't Come Close" to remind that subproblems are separate then combined

mnemonics.title: Master Theorem Cases
mnemonics.content: Case 1: Polynomial Difference - f(n) smaller, Case 2: Same, Case 3: f(n) larger - Remember as "Same, Smaller, Larger"

mnemonics.title: Quick Sort Worst Case
mnemonics.content: QWERT: Quick Sort Worst case occurs when array is Already Sorted or Reverse Sorted, or when pivot is always extreme element

acronyms.usage: Converts time domain signal to frequency domain in O(n log n)
Transforms discrete signal to its frequency components in O(n²)

acronyms.expansion: Minimum Viable Product - Not relevant here
acronyms.usage: Actually: Strassen uses M1-M7 to remember 7 multiplications

visualTips.visualization: Think of sorting cards by dividing into halves repeatedly until single cards, then merging back in sorted order like merging two sorted card piles
Visualize a partition wall (pivot) with smaller elements on left and larger on right - like organizing people by height with a pivot person as reference
Imagine a vertical line splitting points, and a strip of width 2d around it - only points in this strip need to be compared vertically

analogies.analogy: Like managing a large company: break it into departments (divide), fix each department (conquer), then integrate them back (combine)
Like finding a word in dictionary: open middle, decide which half contains word, repeat - instead of searching page by page
Like organizing a deck of cards: spread them out, sort pairs, sort groups of 4, then merge sorted groups together
